suggestions: Task 5 (notifications) references PR URL from Task 4 output in its completion notification payload, but does not declare Task 4 as a dependency — either add Task 4 as a soft dependency or document that the PR URL field is nullable when Task 4 has not completed, to make the data flow contract explicit at the architectural level.

Task 7 subtasks 7003-7004 (auth/JWT) introduce a new backend API surface (`POST /api/auth/login`, `/api/auth/refresh`) that does not exist in Task 2's scope — split this into a dedicated subtask under Task 2 for the auth endpoint implementation on cto-pm, and have Task 7's auth subtasks depend on it, to avoid a cross-layer responsibility gap where the frontend task implicitly requires backend changes.

Tasks 9 and 10 contain subtasks that could be parallelized more aggressively — specifically, 9004 (default-deny NetworkPolicy) has no real dependency on 9001 (HA scaling) but 9005/9006 correctly depend on 9004; similarly 10001 (RBAC), 10003 (secret rotation config), 10005 (audit policy), and 10006 (app-level audit) are all independent foundations that could run in parallel, which the plan already captures via empty dependencies on some but could make more explicit.

Add explicit tasks to define/confirm external service contracts and required endpoints before implementation: (a) discover and document discord-bridge-http payload schema + endpoint paths, (b) linear-bridge payload schema, (c) Hermes API contract (/health + /research path + request/response), (d) NOUS auth/header + endpoint; make these tasks dependencies for Tasks 3 and 5 (and partially 8).

Unblock E2E by adding missing backend/API surface tasks that current tests assume: implement pipeline intake/status endpoints used by 8005 (`POST /api/pipeline/intake`, `GET /api/pipeline/:runId/status`) and a notifications record endpoint assumed by 8004 (`GET /api/pipeline/:runId/notifications`) or revise 8004/8008 to use only already-planned interfaces.

Reduce unnecessary sequential dependencies to enable parallel work: let Task 7 depend on Task 2 (delegation/pipeline status endpoints) and Task 6 (validation report) rather than only Task 6; split Task 6 into (i) pipeline runner + report storage/API (6002/6004/6005) and (ii) Linear live verification (6003) so the core pipeline can ship and be demoed even if live Linear verification isn’t available in an environment.

Remove Tasks 7, 9, and 10 entirely from this plan or move them to a separate v2 task list — they are self-declared deferred and together account for ~35 subtasks that do not advance the PRD's stated acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-5). Their presence makes the critical path (Tasks 1-6, 8) harder to track and inflates the perceived scope.

Collapse subtasks 2006 and 3007 (standalone structured logging passes) into their respective implementation subtasks — logging should be an acceptance criterion on subtasks 2003/3005/3006, not a separate deliverable. A subtask that only 'audits and replaces ad-hoc console.log calls' after the fact is rework, not forward progress.
